sections.content:
Interesting! The right panel now shows white cells where it previously showed gray cells. Let me check what changed more carefully. Looking at the image, the right panel has some white cells now - specifically the top row (3 cells) and the center cell. So clicking on the left panel affected the right panel!
Let me check exactly what changed between the before and after frames.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
prev_lines = previous_frame.ascii.split('\n')

# Check all row diffs
print("--- All row diffs ---")
for r in range(64):
    if lines[r] != prev_lines[r]:
        diffs = []
        for c in range(min(len(lines[r]), len(prev_lines[r]))):
            if lines[r][c] != prev_lines[r][c]:
                diffs.append(c)
        if diffs:
            print(f"Row {r}: cols {min(diffs)}-{max(diffs)}")
            print(f"  Before: ...{prev_lines[r][min(diffs)-2:max(diffs)+3]}...")
            print(f"  After:  ...{lines[r][min(diffs)-2:max(diffs)+3]}...")

print("\n--- Right panel now ---")
for r in range(47, 64):
    print(f"Row {r}: {lines[r][21:39]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
